```bash

echo "--- health body ---"; head -c 500 /tmp/aca-health.txt; echo; echo "--- root body (first 300) ---"; head -c 300 /tmp/aca-root.txt; echo
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The site is returning HTTP 200 for both / and /health. This suggests the application is responding, but the page content itself may still be empty or failing to render.

The blank page likely stems from a Blazor frontend issue rather than a down container. Check the container logs for static asset errors or circuit failures. A cold-start or scaling issue could also hide the UI.

I will provide a step-by-step guide to pull container logs via Azure CLI and the portal. I will also share the quick health checks I ran and the current status.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Right now Azure looks **up**: `schaerbeek-web` is Running on image `:4a4142e`, `/health` and `/` both return **200**. If the browser still looks empty, it’s likely a frontend/Blazor load issue rather than the container being dead — logs below will still tell you which.

### 1. Quick sanity checks

```bash
az login   # if needed

# App status + current image
az containerapp show -n schaerbeek-web -g schaerbeek-rg \
  --query '{status:properties.runningStatus,image:properties.template.containers[0].image,fqdn:properties.configuration.ingress.fqdn}' -o json

# Live probes (first hit after idle can take 15–45s — minReplicas is 0)
curl -i --max-time 60 https://schaerbeek-web.gentledesert-b97640b1.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io/health
curl -i --max-time 60 https://schaerbeek-web.gentledesert-b97640b1.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io/
```

### 2. Console logs (fastest)

```bash
# Last ~100 lines from the running container
az containerapp logs show -n schaerbeek-web -g schaerbeek-rg --type console --tail 100

# Follow live while you refresh the site in the browser
az containerapp logs show -n schaerbeek-web -g schaerbeek-rg --type console --follow
```

Also useful: system logs (scale / pull / probe failures):

```bash
az containerapp logs show -n schaerbeek-web -g schaerbeek-rg --type system --tail 50
```

### 3. Revisions (did CD deploy a bad image?)

```bash
az containerapp revision list -n schaerbeek-web -g schaerbeek-rg -o table
```

Look for `RunningState` / which revision has traffic, and whether the image tag matches the CD run.

### 4. Portal (same data, nicer UI)

1. [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) → resource group **`schaerbeek-rg`** → **`schaerbeek-web`**
2. **Monitoring → Log stream** — live console output  
3. **Application → Revisions and replicas** — replica status / restart reasons  
4. **Monitoring → Logs** (Log Analytics) — historical queries, e.g.:

```kusto
ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL
| where ContainerAppName_s == "schaerbeek-web"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
```

### 5. If the page is blank in the browser

With `/` returning HTML, open DevTools → **Network** and **Console** on the live URL. Check whether CSS/JS (`*.css`, `*.js`, `_framework/...`) fail (404/CORS). Paste any red console errors or failed asset URLs here.

### 6. CD side

In GitHub → **Actions** → **CD**: confirm the **Deploy to Azure Container Apps** job ran (needs `AZURE_DEPLOY_ENABLED=true`). If only **publish** ran, Azure wouldn’t have changed.

---

**What I already see:** app is healthy and serving HTML; console logs are mostly EF polling `outbound_notifications` (noisy but not a crash).  

What does “doesn’t show anything” look like for you — blank white page, spinner forever, browser error, or timeout? If you can share a screenshot or the Network/Console failures, we can narrow it quickly.

---

in the console log I get a Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 ()

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: BASE=&#34;https://schaerbeek-web.gentledesert-b97640b1.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io&#34;
curl -sS &#34;$BASE/&#34; -o /tmp/aca-index.html
echo &#34;=== linked assets ===&#34;
rg -o &#39;href=&#34;[^&#34;]+&#34;|src=&#34;[^&#34;]+&#34;&#39; /tmp/aca-index.html | head -80
echo
echo &#34;=== probing common Blazor paths ===&#34;
for path in \
  &#34;/&#34; \
  &#34;/app.m822hz3gaz.css&#34; \
  &#34;/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web.y2vfqqqz2f.styles.css&#34; \
  &#34;/_framework/blazor.web.js&#34; \
  &#34;/_framework/blazor.webassembly.js&#34; \
  &#34;/_content/MudBlazor/MudBlazor.min.css&#34; \
  &#34;/_content/MudBlazor/MudBlazor.min.js&#34; \
  &#34;/favicon.ico&#34; \
  &#34;/manifest.webmanifest&#34;
do
  code=$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w &#34;%{http_code}&#34; --max-time 20 &#34;$BASE$path&#34;)
  echo &#34;$code  $path&#34;
done
echo
echo &#34;=== extract all local asset refs and probe ===&#34;
python3 &lt;&lt;&#39;PY&#39;
import re, urllib.request, ssl
base=&#34;https://schaerbeek-web.gentledesert-b97640b1.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io&#34;
html=open(&#34;/tmp/aca-index.html&#34;).read()
refs=set(re.findall(r&#39;(?:href|src)=&#34;([^&#34;]+)&#34;&#39;, html))
# also importmaps / modulepreload
refs |= set(re.findall(r&#39;url\(([^)]+)\)&#39;, html))
ctx=ssl.create_default_context()
for r in sorted(refs):
    if r.startswith((&#34;http://&#34;,&#34;https://&#34;,&#34;data:&#34;,&#34;#&#34;,&#34;mailto:&#34;)): 
        continue
    if r.startswith(&#34;//&#34;):
        continue
    path=r if r.startswith(&#34;/&#34;) else &#34;/&#34;+r
    try:
        req=urllib.request.Request(base+path, method=&#34;HEAD&#34;)
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=20) as resp:
            print(f&#34;{resp.status}  {path}&#34;)
    except Exception as e:
        code=getattr(getattr(e,&#39;code&#39;,None), &#39;__str__&#39;, lambda: None)()
        if hasattr(e,&#39;code&#39;):
            print(f&#34;{e.code}  {path}&#34;)
        else:
            # fallback GET
            try:
                with urllib.request.urlopen(base+path, context=ctx, timeout=20) as resp:
                    print(f&#34;{resp.status}  {path}&#34;)
            except Exception as e2:
                if hasattr(e2,&#39;code&#39;):
                    print(f&#34;{e2.code}  {path}&#34;)
                else:
                    print(f&#34;ERR {path} {e2}&#34;)
PY</summary>
```bash
BASE="https://schaerbeek-web.gentledesert-b97640b1.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io"
curl -sS "$BASE/" -o /tmp/aca-index.html
echo "=== linked assets ==="
rg -o 'href="[^"]+"|src="[^"]+"' /tmp/aca-index.html | head -80
echo
echo "=== probing common Blazor paths ==="
for path in \
  "/" \
  "/app.m822hz3gaz.css" \
  "/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web.y2vfqqqz2f.styles.css" \
  "/_framework/blazor.web.js" \
  "/_framework/blazor.webassembly.js" \
  "/_content/MudBlazor/MudBlazor.min.css" \
  "/_content/MudBlazor/MudBlazor.min.js" \
  "/favicon.ico" \
  "/manifest.webmanifest"
do
  code=$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 20 "$BASE$path")
  echo "$code  $path"
done
echo
echo "=== extract all local asset refs and probe ==="
python3 <<'PY'
import re, urllib.request, ssl
base="https://schaerbeek-web.gentledesert-b97640b1.westeurope.azurecontainerapps.io"
html=open("/tmp/aca-index.html").read()
refs=set(re.findall(r'(?:href|src)="([^"]+)"', html))
# also importmaps / modulepreload
refs |= set(re.findall(r'url\(([^)]+)\)', html))
ctx=ssl.create_default_context()
for r in sorted(refs):
    if r.startswith(("http://","https://","data:","#","mailto:")): 
        continue
    if r.startswith("//"):
        continue
    path=r if r.startswith("/") else "/"+r
    try:
        req=urllib.request.Request(base+path, method="HEAD")
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=20) as resp:
            print(f"{resp.status}  {path}")
    except Exception as e:
        code=getattr(getattr(e,'code',None), '__str__', lambda: None)()
        if hasattr(e,'code'):
            print(f"{e.code}  {path}")
        else:
            # fallback GET
            try:
                with urllib.request.urlopen(base+path, context=ctx, timeout=20) as resp:
                    print(f"{resp.status}  {path}")
            except Exception as e2:
                if hasattr(e2,'code'):
                    print(f"{e2.code}  {path}")
                else:
                    print(f"ERR {path} {e2}")
PY
```
